description_long: This is a round, bulbous vase with a wide opening and a visibly uneven, chipped rim, suggesting age or wear. The surface is matte and predominantly grayish with subtle patches of lighter and darker tones, possibly from weathering or mineral deposits. The body is thick and robust, with no visible handles or decorative embellishments, giving it a utilitarian appearance. The rim is irregular, with several noticeable chips and missing sections, exposing the thickness of the ceramic material. The interior appears dark and rough, consistent with hand-formed pottery. Overall, the vase exudes an ancient, rustic character, likely crafted from fired clay.
description: A round, grayish, chipped ceramic vase with a wide, uneven opening and a weathered, matte surface.
description_view_0: The vase is shown with its most damaged rim section visible, highlighting several large chips and a patchy surface.
description_view_1: This view shows a slightly less damaged rim, with subtle color variations and a smoother curvature on the body.
description_view_2: The rim damage is less pronounced here, and the body appears more uniformly rounded with minimal surface markings.
description_view_3: This angle presents the most intact side of the rim, with a smoother, continuous curve and fewer visible chips.

license_info.creator_display_name: The Royal Museums of Art and History
